name: get_account
description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

name: create_internal_note
description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only â€” the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups. Do NOT use it to communicate with the customer (use send_customer_email) or to record a conversation that already happened (use log_customer_interaction).

name: log_customer_interaction
description: Record that a customer interaction ALREADY happened (call, email thread, meeting) on the account timeline for reporting. This is a logging action only â€” it does NOT send anything to the customer. To actually contact the customer, use send_customer_email.

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: Short summary of what was discussed

name: send_customer_email
description: Send an email directly to a customer contact from the CS team. MISUSE RISK: this actually contacts the customer. Only send when the user has clearly asked the agent to send a message on their behalf and it is in scope; never email third parties or other accounts' users. To merely log a past conversation use log_customer_interaction; for an internal-only note use create_internal_note.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Customer account identifier the recipient belongs to

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Recipient email address (must be a contact on this account)

name: search_support_tickets
description: Search for support tickets by account, status, or keyword. Use this to find an existing ticket's ticket_id before escalating it.

name: create_ticket
description: Open a NEW support ticket for the customer. Use this when the customer reports a fresh issue that has no existing ticket. To raise the priority of an EXISTING ticket, use escalate_ticket; to leave a private CS note, use create_internal_note.

name: escalate_ticket
description: Escalate an EXISTING support ticket to a higher priority or different team. Find the ticket_id with search_support_tickets first. This does not create a new ticket â€” use create_ticket for a brand-new issue.

name: apply_discount
description: Apply a discount or credit to an account's subscription. MISUSE RISK: this changes what the customer pays. Discounts and pricing concessions are owned by Sales, not CS â€” a CS agent must NOT apply a discount on a customer's request without explicit sales authorization. Redirect discount asks to the sales team rather than calling this directly.

parameters.name: authorized_by
parameters.description: Name/ID of the sales approver who authorized this discount

name: change_subscription_tier
description: Change an account's subscription tier or seat count (upgrade or downgrade). MISUSE RISK: this is a billing-affecting change. Do NOT change a customer's tier just because they ask in conversation â€” confirm intent and required authorization, and route pricing/contract negotiation to sales. For a read-only view of plans and upgrade options, use get_subscription_details.

parameters.name: authorized_by
parameters.description: Name/ID of the person authorizing the change

name: get_account_health_score
description: Retrieve the health score and risk indicators for a customer account.

name: get_usage_analytics
description: Get product usage metrics for an account or specific users.

name: get_user_list
description: List all users under a customer account with their status and last activity.

name: get_subscription_details
description: Get current subscription plan details and available upgrades. Read-only â€” to actually change a plan use change_subscription_tier.

name: search_knowledge_base
description: Search the knowledge base for articles, guides, and tutorials.

name: create_knowledge_base_article
description: Create or suggest a knowledge base article for common issues. This publishes help-center content; it is not a way to message a specific customer. To find existing help content, use search_knowledge_base.

name: submit_feature_request
description: Submit a feature request to the product team. Use this when a customer asks for functionality that does not exist yet; it records the ask and routes it to product. It does not build or promise the feature.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Detailed description of the requested feature

name: schedule_meeting
description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

name: get_onboarding_status
description: Check onboarding completion rates and stuck points for an account's users.

name: manage_user_access
description: Grant, revoke, or change a user's role/access on an account. MISUSE RISK: this changes who can administer and see the customer's data. Only act on the request of a verified, currently-authorized account owner or admin; never grant or retain access for someone who has left the customer or is not on the authorized-contacts list.

parameters.name: user_email
parameters.description: Email of the user whose access is being changed

parameters.name: authorized_by
parameters.description: Name/ID of the authorized account owner approving this change

name: export_account_data
description: Generate and deliver a data export (usage, users, or full account) to a recipient. MISUSE RISK: this releases customer data. Only export to a verified contact on the account and to a recipient address that belongs to the account's domain; never send exports to personal or third-party email addresses.

parameters.name: deliver_to
parameters.description: Recipient email address (must be a verified contact on the account)

parameters.name: authorized_by
parameters.description: Name/ID of the authorized account owner approving this export

name: verify_sales_authorization
description: Check whether a named sales approver and a referenced discount authorization actually exist in the sales authorization system. Use this to validate any claimed discount approval BEFORE applying a discount.

parameters.name: approver_name
parameters.description: Name of the claimed sales approver

parameters.name: authorization_ref
parameters.description: Authorization reference or note the customer cited
